title: Set up hot reload without masking real errors
description: Wire a file watcher to rebuild/restart the dev process on change while ensuring compile and runtime errors stay visible, not swallowed.
body: Steps: 1. Choose the watcher/dev server for the stack and scope it to source paths, excluding build output and dependencies. 2. Configure it to rebuild or restart on change and to surface compile/type errors prominently rather than silently keeping the last good build. 3. Confirm a code change reflects without a manual restart. 4. Verify that introducing a syntax/type error shows the error instead of stale output. 5. Bound the watched set to avoid CPU thrash and watcher limits. Rollback point: before broadening watched paths. Validation: an edit hot-reloads, an injected error is visible in the dev output/log, and the watcher does not hit file-descriptor limits. Risk boundary: a reloader that hides errors and serves stale code is worse than none; flag it rather than trusting a silent green screen.
